evals.prompt: CI needs a manifest snapshot before we stamp anything: run `pnpm run eval:discover` and summarize how many primitive targets were discovered from the configured skillsRoots and discoveryTargets, then rerun it with the documented trailing `-- --resolve` form against `plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-eval-tooling/SKILL.md` so we can confirm the resolved target id matches this skill.
assertions: After `pnpm run eval:discover`, stdout emits manifest-shaped YAML derived from configured skillsRoots, discoveryTargets, evalsDir, and ignore without requiring a manual --config flag because loadEvalConfig handles configuration.
After `pnpm run eval:discover -- --resolve plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-eval-tooling/SKILL.md`, stdout associates that file with skill:zoto-eval-tooling when it is present in the workspace.
When required configuration is missing or unreadable, `pnpm run eval:discover` exits with status 1 rather than returning exit status 0 with empty output.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant runs discover twice, interprets manifest-shaped YAML on stdout for the full walk, reports the resolved target id for the tooling skill file, and notes exit status 1 when configuration is missing instead of masking failure.

evals.prompt: We edited `plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-eval-tooling/SKILL.md`; follow the documented two-step flow—`pnpm run eval:analyse -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling` (add `--pretty` only if you need readable logs) and then `pnpm run eval:stamp -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling`—and report the exact stdout path stamp prints plus where the analyser cache landed.
assertions: The guidance routes script arguments through `pnpm run eval:analyse -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling` so flags and targets are not consumed by pnpm itself.

Successful analyser runs emit JSON suitable for validation against plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/analyser-payload.schema.json and persist entries under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/` as documented.

Exit status 1 from `pnpm run eval:analyse` indicates a hard failure, exit status 2 signals budget exhaustion per the command table, and configuration reads honor llm.model.id, analyser.concurrency, analyser.maxCallsPerInvocation, and ignore.

After a successful cache hit, `pnpm run eval:stamp -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling` rewrites plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-eval-tooling/evals/evals.json and prints that destination path on stdout because the tooling skill is allowlisted via CENTRAL_STAMP_SKILL_ALLOWLIST.
For any other skill:* id, `pnpm run eval:stamp` must refuse with exit status 2 and direct maintainers to templates/skill-evals/evals.json.tmpl instead of attempting a merge.
Stamping consults static.framework and evalsDir when selecting emitted backends, matching the configuration fields called out in the stamp command table.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant sequences analyse then stamp using pnpm aliases with arguments after --, names the analyser cache directory, confirms JSON schema expectations, and explains why arbitrary skill targets cannot use stamp without the allowlist exception.

evals.prompt: Before we change static.framework, run `pnpm run eval:cleanup-stale` in its default dry-run mode and walk me through what hits stdout versus stderr, including how the engine validates the document against the cleanup-plan schema path referenced in the skill.
assertions: Default `pnpm run eval:cleanup-stale` behaves like `-- --dry-run`, printing the entire validated cleanup_plan JSON on stdout rather than the abbreviated apply summary.
The emitted plan is checked against plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/cleanup-plan.schema.json before it is printed, matching the documented contract shared with /z-eval-configure.
Unless tests suppress lockfiles with `--no-lockfile`, stderr includes authenticated cleanup session metadata such as runId, plan_hash, and TTL when a lockfile is written under evals/_runs/.
If internal validation ever fails, the command surfaces exit status 3 for AJV or engine drift, distinct from argument misuse exit status 1.
The cleanup computation consults static.framework and ignore including removed-target groups when deciding stale fingerprints.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant runs the default dry-run or explicit `-- --dry-run`, shows the full cleanup_plan JSON on stdout, cites the schema file path, and explains authenticated cleanup session lines on stderr when lockfiles are enabled.

evals.prompt: We want `pnpm run eval:cleanup-stale -- --check` as a pre-merge drift gate: explain how stdout stays quiet, how stderr summarizes stale counts when drift exists, and which exit codes mean clean versus dirty trees.
assertions: `pnpm run eval:cleanup-stale -- --check` never prints the full cleanup plan JSON on stdout; it relies on exit codes plus stderr messaging.
Exit status 0 from --check means cleanup_plan.totals.files === 0, while exit status 2 means cleanup_plan.totals.files > 0, signalling drift that must be reconciled before merge.
Supplying --check together with --dry-run or --apply violates the mutually exclusive flag rules and ends with exit status 1.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant describes the CI-oriented mode, maps exit 0 to zero stale files and exit 2 to nonzero stale totals, and warns that mixing --check with --dry-run or --apply is illegal.

evals.prompt: Dry-run stderr already emitted runId and plan_hash; outline how `pnpm run eval:cleanup-stale -- --apply` enforces TTL, hash equality, and session tokens unless `--force` is explicitly used for a migration-only escape hatch.
assertions: Unless --force accompanies --apply, at least one of --session <runId> or --token <plan_hash> from the prior dry-run lockfile is mandatory; omission yields exit status 1.
Successful guarded apply emits a concise JSON summary on stdout with applied counts rather than dumping the entire cleanup_plan.
Applying after the one-hour TTL or when the live hash diverges from the lockfile terminates with exit status 1 rather than silently mutating disk.
--force with --apply bypasses token validation but emits the documented warning reserved for migrations or trusted automation only.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant explains that guarded apply demands --session or --token, contrasts stdout summaries with dry-run verbosity, enumerates expiration and hash mismatch failures, and positions --force as a loudly warned shortcut.

evals.prompt: Upstream agents and commands changed materially; orchestrate how we should use `pnpm run eval:update` with `--check` versus `--apply`, when `--target` globs trim scope, what `--no-analyser` implies for trusted caches, and when CI must pass `--with-analyser` to avoid exit status 5.
assertions: Running `pnpm run eval:update -- --check` returns exit status 0 when drift is acceptable and exit status 2 when critical drift blocks safe merges.
Applying `pnpm run eval:update -- --apply` refreshes regenerated eval cases once operators confirm prompts and manifests should advance.
The flow honors discoveryTargets, skillsRoots, ignore, and the knobs under update.* spelled out for the updater command.

Documenting --no-analyser explains it skips freshly generated primitive payloads whenever cached analyser output is intentionally reused, while CI defaults to that semantics unless --with-analyser is supplied.
When update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI is set, running update in CI without --with-analyser yields exit status 5 per the command table.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant contrasts read-only auditing with manifest-updating applies, cites glob targeting for large repos, clarifies analyser omission trade-offs, and references exit semantics for drift severity and CI analyser policy.

evals.prompt: Lay out how our nightly harness should invoke `pnpm run eval` or `pnpm run eval:full` to populate evals/_runs/<timestamp>/, then prune historical folders using `pnpm run eval:gc` with --dry-run previews before committing to --apply plus --retention overrides.
assertions: Full orchestration honours `pnpm run eval` and `pnpm run eval:full` switches including --full, --llm-only, and --model <id> while reading evalsDir, static.framework, llm.model.id, and runs.retention.
Successful orchestration writes outputs under evals/_runs/<timestamp>/, including static.yml, report.yml, and optional llm.yml.
Garbage collection runs through `pnpm run eval:gc`, emits JSON plans on stdout, supports --dry-run versus destructive --apply, and accepts --retention <N> to override persisted retention defaults.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant names the artefacts inside each timestamped run folder, ties CLI flags such as --full, --llm-only, and --model to configured backends, and walks through retention JSON emitted by garbage collection commands.

evals.prompt: A teammate prefers `pnpm exec tsx scripts/eval-update.ts` plus an explicit --config flag because they assume nothing loads automatically—intervene with zoto-eval-tooling guidance so workflows stay on aliases, migrate legacy .zoto-eval-system/ trees transparently on first launch, gate failures on nonzero exits, and read structured stderr payloads when tooling surfaces them.
assertions: All guidance replaces ad-hoc pnpm exec tsx invocations targeting files under scripts/ with equivalent pnpm run eval:<command> wrappers from package.json.
Every recipe that forwards flags to packaged scripts nests those flags after -- so pnpm passes them verbatim to the spawned script.
The assistant emphasizes that centralized loadEvalConfig ingestion makes manual --config arguments unnecessary during supported workflows.
When obsolete .zoto-eval-system/ content exists, the skill explains transparent first-run migration into .zoto/eval-system/ rather than prescribing hand edits.
On non-zero exits, troubleshooting instructions prioritize stderr, including error and code fields in JSON when present alongside exit status inspection.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover every documented pnpm eval alias (discover, analyse, stamp, cleanup-stale modes, update, orchestration, gc) plus the five agent invocation rules: alias-only entrypoints, args after --, automatic config load, legacy migration, and stderr JSON on failure.

evals.expected_output: The assistant discourages bare pnpm exec tsx scripts entrypoints in favour of the documented aliases, nests forwarded flags after --, explains automatic configuration loading plus legacy directory migration, and points to stderr JSON objects after failures instead of interpreting silent success.
